title: STDIO, Streamable HTTP, Lifecycle, and Deployment · Spiral Review
summary: Choose and configure a transport for local and remote MCP servers. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a local and remote transport deployment plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement newline-safe STDIO transport without logging protocol noise to standard output.
Implement remote Streamable HTTP with correct routing, content types, and transport security.
Handle startup, initialization, shutdown, timeout, and reconnect behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

stimulus.title: Observability case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext transports. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Complete request bodies should always be logged for debugging.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Log identifiers, latency, errors, and side effects without leaking protocol secrets. Artifact under review: a local and remote transport deployment plan.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Observability, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Observability applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Log identifiers, latency, errors, and side effects without leaking protocol secrets.
